Forensic Sciences is a name given to the technical services carried out in the detection and proof of crime and criminality by examining and evaluating the material evidence obtained during judicial and administrative investigations and covers all branches of science used for the purpose of justice.

The success of the prevention and elucidation of all kinds of crimes, especially terrorism, human trafficking, child abuse, cyber crimes, illegal drug and arms trafficking, bribery and forgery, directly depends on the existence of qualified manpower working in the field of forensic sciences, crime scene investigation at international standards and quality, as well as the analysis of evidence collected from the crime scene, victims, suspects and witnesses with modern information and technologies.

Professionals who will work in the field of forensic sciences should be trained in a way that will not only transfer information and technology from developed countries, but also develop new tools, methods and technologies to save our country from foreign dependency and to obtain patents.

Evidence collection activities and evidence examination carried out in order to improve the quality of justice services are not only a technical issue, but also require the correct evaluation and interpretation of the results obtained.

The Forensic Sciences Practice Hall is equipped with the necessary technology and infrastructure to train people who know how to think interdisciplinary, have a strong and reliable scientific basis, and have a solid laboratory experience.
